summary: Matched the Target Test Pikachu ground/stage module by replacing asm/melee/gr/grtpikachu.s with C in src/melee/gr/grtpikachu.c and adding a corresponding header. The build configuration was switched from the assembly object to the C object, and configure.py now marks melee/gr/grtpikachu.c as Matching. The C reconstruction covers stage data, callback tables, stage initialization, ground object setup, simple no-op/boolean callbacks, animation setup, and cleanup/update callbacks.
what_changed: Added a full C implementation in src/melee/gr/grtpikachu.c for grTPikachu_80222E7C through grTPikachu_80223158.
Defined the StageCallbacks table grTPk_803E9270 in C, preserving three callback entries plus a NULL terminator entry.
Defined StageData grTPk_803E92CC in C with stage id 57, data path "/GrTPk.dat", module lifecycle callbacks, item/anim callbacks, and flags value (1 << 0).

Implemented grTPikachu_80222E80 to set stage_info.unk8C.b4 false and b5 true, spawn/setup ground ids 0, 1, and 2, then call the standard Ground_801C39C0, Ground_801C3BB4, Ground_801C4210, and Ground_801C42AC initialization helpers.

Implemented grTPikachu_80222F20 as the shared ground-object creation/setup helper using Ground_801C14D0, HSD_GObjGetUserData, GObj_SetupGXLink, optional StageCallbacks dispatch, optional HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54 installation, and OSReport on missing gobj.
Implemented animation/setup callbacks that call grAnime_801C8138, Ground_801C2ED0, lb_800115F4, and Ground_801C2FE0 where indicated by the original assembly.
Added src/melee/gr/grtpikachu.h with prototypes and forward includes for platform, lb, dolphin mtx, and baselib types.
Changed configure.py from Object(NonMatching, "melee/gr/grtpikachu.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/gr/grtpikachu.c").
Changed obj_files.mk to build $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/gr/grtpikachu.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/gr/grtpikachu.s.o.
smart_moves: Used existing shared stage abstractions, especially StageCallbacks and StageData, instead of leaving raw data arrays or opaque .4byte data.
Reconstructed bitfield writes to stage_info.unk8C as named bit assignments, stage_info.unk8C.b4 = false and stage_info.unk8C.b5 = true, which makes the original rlwimi byte manipulation readable.
Factored gobj user data through HSD_GObjGetUserData and Ground* fields, matching the assembly's offsets while improving type clarity.
Preserved callback-table behavior directly: callback3 becomes gp->x1C_callback, callback0 is invoked immediately when non-NULL, and callback2 is installed as a process with priority 4.
Used GET_GROUND(gobj) and PAD_STACK(8) in the callbacks involving Ground_801C2ED0 and grAnime_801C8138, likely to satisfy matching stack/codegen constraints while following project idioms.
Kept the original OSReport string and source line value used by the assembly error path, including "grtpikachu.c" and line 195.
Moved the object in both configure.py and obj_files.mk, ensuring the matched C file actually replaces the asm object in all relevant build paths.
decomp_lessons: For small target-test stage modules, the assembly often maps cleanly onto a StageData definition, a StageCallbacks array, and repeated boilerplate callback functions.
Stage callback tables with five-word records can be represented as StageCallbacks entries, including a final sentinel entry of all NULL/0 values.
Initialization code that mutates stage_info.unk8C bits should be checked against known bitfield names rather than left as byte masks when the struct supports it.

A shared object-spawn helper can be reconstructed by following the Ground_801C14D0 result path: get Ground user data, clear callbacks, set GX link, copy optional callback fields, invoke init callback, and install proc callback.

Empty functions and boolean stubs should still be typed according to their callback-table roles; this PR uses void no-ops, bool false returns, a NULL lb_UnkAnimStruct* return, and a final bool true return.
When replacing an asm file with matched C, update both the object list and the matching/nonmatching classification; deleting the asm alone is not sufficient.
naming_conventions: Preserved address-based function names with the module prefix grTPikachu_8022xxxx.
Preserved data symbol names with the short stage prefix grTPk, such as grTPk_803E9270 and grTPk_803E92CC.
Header guard used lowercase module style: _grtpikachu_h_.
The stage data file path remains the original short asset path string: "/GrTPk.dat".
Local variable names are conventional and type-oriented: gobj for HSD_GObj*, gp for Ground*, cb for StageCallbacks*, joint for HSD_JObj*.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced raw assembly data for grTPk_803E9270 with a typed StageCallbacks array whose layout matches the original .4byte sequence.
Replaced raw assembly data for grTPk_803E92CC with a typed StageData initializer.
Used bool parameters and returns for stubs that compile to li r3, 0 / blr or li r3, 1 / blr where appropriate.
Used explicit NULL checks before callback invocation and process creation to mirror the original branch structure.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in grTPikachu_80223044 and grTPikachu_802230D4, indicating stack-shape padding was needed for matching around the Ground_801C2ED0 and grAnime_801C8138 calls.
Preserved the OSReport failure path in grTPikachu_80222F20, including format string, source filename string, and line constant.
Used false as the third argument to grAnime_801C8138 in places where the assembly passed 0.
follow_up_queries: Find other target-test stage modules adjacent to grtpikachu, such as grtpichu or grtpurin, for reusable StageCallbacks/StageData patterns.
Search for PAD_STACK(8) in stage callback functions to identify similar matching requirements around Ground_801C2ED0 and grAnime_801C8138.
Search for OSReport strings of the form "%s:%d: couldn t get gobj(id=%d)\n" to compare shared ground object setup helpers.
Compare grTPikachu_80222F20 against equivalent setup helpers in other grt* modules for opportunities to infer better names or common abstractions.
